---
title: "Resources"
---

## Guides

Step-by-step instructions for common tasks in this course.

| Guide | When to use it |
|-------|---------------|
| [Setting Up an R Project](r-projects.qmd) | Every assignment — start here |
| [Using R Scripts for Assignments](r-scripts.qmd) | Assignments 1–2 |
| [Using Quarto Documents for Assignments](quarto-docs.qmd) | Assignments 3–8 and Final Project |
| [Common R Errors (and What They Mean)](common-errors.qmd) | When you get a red error message and don't know what it means |

## Session Handouts

Printable reference sheets for each class session — key functions, syntax, and tips on one page.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Weeks 1–3

| Session | Handout |
|---------|---------|
| 2: Your First Visualization | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-02-first-visualization.pdf) |
| 3: Data Transformation I | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-03-data-transformation-1.pdf) |
| 4: Data Transformation II | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-04-data-transformation-2.pdf) |
| 5: Data Tidying | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-05-data-tidying.pdf) |
| 6: Data Import | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-06-data-import.pdf) |

### Weeks 4–6

| Session | Handout |
|---------|---------|
| 7: Quarto & Reproducibility | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-07-quarto.pdf) |
| 8: Layers & Aesthetics | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-08-layers-aesthetics.pdf) |
| 9: Perception & Design | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-09-perception-design.pdf) |
| 10: EDA — Variation | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-10-eda-variation.pdf) |
| 11: EDA — Covariation | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-11-eda-covariation.pdf) |
| 12: Data Types | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-12-data-types.pdf) |

### Weeks 7–10

| Session | Handout |
|---------|---------|
| 13: Strings, Factors & Text | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-13-strings-factors.pdf) |
| 14: Joins | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-14-joins.pdf) |
| 15: Missing Data | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-15-missing-data.pdf) |
| 16: Storytelling with Data | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-16-storytelling.pdf) |
| 17: Correlation & Regression | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-17-correlation-regression.pdf) |
| 18: Putting It All Together | [PDF](/files/handouts-pdf/handout-18-putting-it-together.pdf) |

### Other Handouts

| Handout | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| [APA Figure Guidelines](/files/handouts-pdf/apa-figure-guidelines.pdf) | Formatting figures for journal submissions |
| [Data Visualization Principles](/files/handouts-pdf/data-viz-principles.pdf) | Key design principles at a glance |

:::

## Course Textbook

- [R for Data Science (2nd edition)](https://r4ds.hadley.nz/) — free online. Weekly readings are listed on the [schedule](/schedule.qmd).

## Cheatsheets

One-page visual references for the packages we use most. Print these out or keep them open while you work.

- [ggplot2 Cheatsheet](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/data-visualization.pdf) — geoms, aesthetics, scales, themes
- [dplyr Cheatsheet](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/data-transformation.pdf) — filter, select, mutate, summarize, group_by
- [tidyr Cheatsheet](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/tidyr.pdf) — pivot_longer, pivot_wider, separate, unite
- [RStudio IDE Cheatsheet](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/rstudio-ide.pdf) — keyboard shortcuts, panes, project setup
- [Quarto Cheatsheet](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/quarto.pdf) — code chunks, Markdown formatting, YAML options
- [Browse all cheatsheets](https://posit.co/resources/cheatsheets/)

## Extra Practice

If you want more practice beyond the assignments, these are all free.

- [Posit Primers](https://posit.cloud/learn/primers) — interactive R tutorials in your browser. Great for reinforcing the basics.
- [Cédric Scherer's ggplot2 Tutorial](https://www.cedricscherer.com/2019/08/05/a-ggplot2-tutorial-for-beautiful-plotting-in-r/) — a beautifully illustrated deep dive into ggplot2. Useful when you're working on the visualization assignments.
- [Quarto Tutorial](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/hello/rstudio.html) — Quarto's own getting-started guide, if you want more detail than our in-class introduction.

## Finding Datasets

For your final project, you'll need a psychology or social science dataset. Here are good places to look:

- [Open Science Framework (OSF)](https://osf.io/) — published research data, often with codebooks
- [ICPSR](https://www.icpsr.umich.edu/) — large archive of social and behavioral science data
- [General Social Survey (GSS)](https://gss.norc.org/) — decades of survey data on American attitudes and behaviors
- [World Values Survey](https://www.worldvaluessurvey.org/) — cross-cultural survey data
- [Kaggle Datasets](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets) — wide variety; filter for social science topics
- Your own research lab (with advisor permission)

See the [Final Project page](/project/index.qmd) for dataset requirements.

## Software

- [Download R](https://cran.r-project.org/)
- [Download RStudio](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)

## Getting Help

- **In class:** Start assignments during work time — the instructor and TA are there to help
- **Office hours:** See the [syllabus](/syllabus.qmd) for times and location
- **Canvas discussion board:** Post questions anytime — classmates and the teaching team can respond
- **Classmates:** Work together, but write your own code
